extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won Stage 17 atop Angliru, outsprinting teammate VINGEGAARD despite the latter defending GC, narrowing gap to leader KUSS to just 8 seconds
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL attacked early after deliberately conceding time previous day, forcing Jumbo-Visma to control despite teammate leading GC
UAE's SOLER attacked first climb forcing Jumbo response, revealing UAE's willingness to exploit Jumbo's internal GC complications
ROGLIČ outsprinted VINGEGAARD at finish despite teammate KUSS holding red jersey by seconds, exposing team priority tensions
KUSS finished 19 seconds behind teammates, suggesting deliberate pacing choice or inability to match their final acceleration

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Posted second-fastest Angliru ascent ever while taking stage, now only 8 seconds from red despite supporting role
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Matched Roglič on Angliru climb, moved to 29 seconds behind Kuss, not respecting internal team hierarchy
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: Lost 19 seconds to teammates on final climb, red jersey lead down to 8 seconds reveals vulnerability on ultra-steep finales
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Attacked breakaway after deliberately losing time previous day, signaling GC abandonment enables stage-hunting freedom rest of race
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: UAE allowed him to attack despite being GC threat, indicating team willing to exploit Jumbo's complicated three-leader situation
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: Finished same time as Vingegaard, moved within striking distance showing late-race form improvement
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Dropped significantly on Angliru, losing podium contention and now 4+ minutes down
